purpose: Remove architectural drift around planning and blueprints. planning_utils.py must remain a technical helper module only. Fachliche planning templates, seed blueprints and role/template metadata should move into catalog/blueprint-backed sources that are used by TemplatePlanningStrategy and propagated into PlanNodes/Tasks.
problem_statement: agent/services/planning_utils.py currently contains GOAL_TEMPLATES, keyword matching and execution-focused fallback templates.
TemplatePlanningStrategy uses match_goal_template(...) from planning_utils, so AutoPlanner planning currently prefers this hardcoded mini-template system before HubCopilot/LLM planning.

The real Blueprint/Team/Role/Template mechanism exists separately in routes/teams.py and services/team_blueprint_service.py, including TeamBlueprintDB, BlueprintRoleDB, BlueprintArtifactDB, RoleDB, TemplateDB and TeamDB.
routes/teams.py still owns large Python seed data blocks for standard blueprints, starter tasks and role/template definitions.

PlanningService currently derives task kind, retrieval hints and capabilities mostly from task title/description instead of consistently carrying blueprint role/template metadata into PlanNodes and Tasks.
This creates multiple sources of fachliche planning truth: seed/team blueprints, hardcoded planning_utils templates and heuristic PlanNode metadata.
non_goals: Do not remove planning_utils parsing, sanitizing, priority normalization, LLM JSON extraction or prompt-injection checks.
Do not break existing AutoPlanner API behavior during migration.
Do not rewrite the entire Team/Blueprint API in this track.
Do not move orchestration or policy decisions into clients.
Do not require DB access for every simple unit test; provide deterministic fixture/catalog loading where needed.
Do not remove existing seed blueprints before exported JSON fixtures and reconciliation tests prove equivalent behavior.
success_criteria: planning_utils.py no longer owns fachliche GOAL_TEMPLATES as the primary template source.
TemplatePlanningStrategy resolves templates through a dedicated planning template catalog or blueprint-backed adapter.
Existing core planning modes such as bug_fix, feature, refactor, test, repo_analysis, sys_diag, code_fix, new_software_project and project_evolution remain covered by tests.
Seed blueprints and starter artifacts are loaded from JSON/YAML data files instead of large Python constants in routes/teams.py.
PlanNodes and materialized Tasks preserve useful blueprint provenance and role/template metadata where available.
planning_utils.py remains focused on technical utilities: sanitizing, validation, JSON extraction, subtask normalization and followup parsing.
Docs explain the relationship between Blueprint/Team/Role/Template definitions, AutoPlanner template planning, PlanNodes and Tasks.
No duplicate client-side or hardcoded ad-hoc planning system remains as the preferred path.

tasks.title: Hardcoded planning templates inventory add
acceptance_criteria: Inventory lists every current GOAL_TEMPLATES entry from agent/services/planning_utils.py.
Inventory records keywords, subtasks, artifact/risk/test/review metadata and current expected behavior.
Inventory marks which entries map cleanly to existing standard blueprints and which need a planning-only catalog entry.
Inventory identifies build_execution_focused_goal_template and EXECUTION_FOCUSED_GOAL_HINTS as separate fallback behavior.

tasks.title: Blueprint mapping decision document add
acceptance_criteria: ADR states that fachliche planning templates belong to a catalog/blueprint-backed path, not planning_utils.py.
ADR defines planning_utils.py as technical utility module only.
ADR explains how Team Blueprints, Role Templates, Blueprint Artifacts and AutoPlanner template planning relate.
ADR defines temporary compatibility behavior for legacy template names and keywords.

tasks.title: Planning template catalog file add
acceptance_criteria: Catalog captures current GOAL_TEMPLATES entries in data form, including keywords and subtasks.
Schema validates template id, keywords, subtasks, priority, depends_on and optional artifact/risk/test/review metadata.
Catalog includes explicit mappings or references to related standard blueprints where applicable.
Schema tests validate minimal and full examples.

tasks.title: Planning template catalog service add
acceptance_criteria: Service loads planning templates from config/planning_templates.json or an injected fixture path for tests.
Service resolves by exact template id and keyword match.
Service returns normalized subtasks compatible with PlanningService._build_nodes.
Unknown templates return None without raising in normal planning flow.
Tests cover exact id match, keyword match, no match, invalid catalog and deterministic ordering.

tasks.title: Blueprint-backed planning adapter add
acceptance_criteria: Adapter can derive starter planning subtasks from TeamBlueprintDB/BlueprintArtifactDB task artifacts when a matching blueprint exists.
Adapter can return blueprint_id, blueprint_name, artifact refs and role/template hints as provenance metadata.
Adapter does not require clients to know blueprint internals.
Adapter fails closed/degraded when DB is unavailable and does not block catalog fallback.
Tests use repository/session fixtures or mocks and cover at least one seed blueprint.

tasks.title: TemplatePlanningStrategy use catalog adapter
acceptance_criteria: TemplatePlanningStrategy no longer calls planning_utils.match_goal_template directly as the primary path.
TemplatePlanningStrategy resolves template subtasks through PlanningTemplateCatalog or blueprint-backed adapter.
Planning order remains Template -> HubCopilot -> LLM unless explicitly changed elsewhere.
Existing AutoPlanner plan_goal tests still pass for common template goals.
Result still marks template_used=true and planning_mode=template when catalog/blueprint template resolves.

tasks.title: planning_utils legacy template API deprecate
acceptance_criteria: GOAL_TEMPLATES is removed from planning_utils.py or kept only as a temporary imported compatibility fixture with explicit deprecation comment.
match_goal_template either delegates to PlanningTemplateCatalog or is deprecated and no longer used by TemplatePlanningStrategy.
planning_utils.py still provides sanitize_input, validate_goal, JSON extraction, subtask normalization and followup parsing.
Tests cover technical utility functions independently from template catalog content.

tasks.title: Execution-focused fallback move out of planning_utils
acceptance_criteria: EXECUTION_FOCUSED_GOAL_HINTS and build_execution_focused_goal_template move to a dedicated fallback module or catalog entry.
Fallback behavior remains deterministic for coding/test-heavy goals.
Fallback is clearly lower priority than exact catalog/blueprint matches.
Tests cover Python/Fibonacci-style fallback without relying on planning_utils GOAL_TEMPLATES.

tasks.title: AutoPlanner compatibility regression tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify old template ids still produce meaningful subtasks: bug_fix, feature, refactor, test, repo_analysis, sys_diag, code_fix, new_software_project, project_evolution.
Tests verify keyword matching still works for representative German and English goal text.
Tests verify no LLM call is made when a catalog/blueprint template resolves.
Tests verify fallback to HubCopilot/LLM still works when no template matches.

tasks.title: Planning blueprint docs update
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain current planning order: catalog/blueprint template, HubCopilot, LLM fallback.
Docs explain that Team Blueprints create teams/starter artifacts while PlanningTemplateCatalog provides AutoPlanner subtask templates.
Docs define how future domain blueprints should provide planning templates without hardcoding Python dictionaries.
Docs mention legacy planning_utils cleanup status.

tasks.title: Release gate planning cleanup check add
acceptance_criteria: Release gate or smoke check catches reintroduction of large hardcoded GOAL_TEMPLATES in planning_utils.py.
Check catches reintroduction of large SEED_BLUEPRINTS or *_INITIAL_TASKS constants in routes/teams.py after seed catalog migration.
Check allows technical utility functions in planning_utils.py.
Check reports actionable failure message pointing to PlanningTemplateCatalog/Blueprint catalog path.
Test covers a synthetic violation.

tasks.title: Seed blueprint catalog JSON extract
acceptance_criteria: Moves current standard seed definitions from routes/teams.py into data files without behavior loss.
Catalog includes Scrum, Scrum-OpenCode, Kanban, Research, Code-Repair, Security-Review, Release-Prep and Research-Evolution if currently present.
Catalog preserves roles, template names, artifact tasks, policy artifacts, sort order and role config.
Schema validates blueprint name, base_team_type_name, roles, artifacts and template references.
Tests validate full seed catalog fixture.

tasks.title: Seed blueprint loader service add
acceptance_criteria: Service loads seed blueprints from config/blueprints/standard/blueprints.json or injected fixture path.
Service normalizes and validates template specs, role specs, blueprint roles and artifacts.
Service exposes deterministic seed blueprint payloads for reconcile_seed_blueprints_service.
Service does not import Flask routes.
Tests cover normal load, missing file, invalid duplicate role/artifact order and deterministic ordering.

tasks.title: routes/teams seed constants replace with loader
acceptance_criteria: routes/teams.py no longer owns large SEED_BLUEPRINTS or *_INITIAL_TASKS data blocks as primary source.
Existing seed reconciliation still creates/updates the same templates, roles, team types, blueprint roles and artifacts.
Existing public endpoints for blueprint catalog/list/detail continue working.
Regression test compares old expected seed names and important artifact counts against loader output.
Route module remains primarily HTTP/API orchestration and delegates seed data loading to service layer.

tasks.title: PlanNode blueprint provenance fields add
acceptance_criteria: PlanNode rationale or explicit fields preserve blueprint_id, blueprint_name, blueprint_artifact_id, blueprint_role_name and template_name when available.
Migration is backwards compatible for existing PlanNodeDB rows.
No client owns or fabricates blueprint provenance.
Tests verify catalog-only and blueprint-backed planning both produce deterministic provenance metadata.

tasks.title: Materialized Task blueprint metadata carry-over
acceptance_criteria: Tasks materialized from PlanNodes carry blueprint provenance in worker_execution_context, history, status details or explicit metadata according to existing TaskDB constraints.
Task metadata includes role/template/capability defaults when available.
Existing task materialization without blueprint provenance still works.
Tests verify metadata is visible to worker/context construction without client-side orchestration.

tasks.title: Role/template defaults influence PlanNode hints
acceptance_criteria: When blueprint role config provides capability_defaults, risk_profile or verification_defaults, PlanningService merges them into PlanNode rationale/verification_spec where safe.
Heuristic title/description capability derivation remains fallback only.
Role/template defaults cannot weaken Hub policy or approval requirements.
Tests cover capability enrichment, verification default enrichment and policy-non-bypass behavior.

execution_order_summary: First inventory the existing hardcoded templates and record the architectural decision.
Extract current planning template data into a validated planning template catalog.
Extract seed blueprint data from routes/teams.py into a validated seed blueprint catalog.
Add catalog services and optional blueprint-backed planning adapter.
Replace routes/teams.py seed constants with loader-backed reconciliation.
Propagate blueprint role/template provenance into PlanNodes and materialized Tasks.
Deprecate/remove fachliche GOAL_TEMPLATES from planning_utils while keeping technical utilities.
Add compatibility tests, docs and release-gate checks so behavior remains stable.
scope_rules: Hub remains control plane for orchestration and policy decisions; worker-only execution model stays intact.
No worker-to-worker orchestration is introduced while moving planning templates into catalogs/adapters.
Catalog/blueprint-backed planning must not weaken approval gates, command policy or review requirements.
Migration is additive and backward-compatible first; no big-bang replacement of planner behavior.
Blueprint provenance metadata is generated in backend services, never fabricated in client surfaces.
summary_notes: This cleanup should be done before large new domain blueprint work if possible, because Blender/FreeCAD/KiCad should not add more hardcoded Python planning dictionaries.
The safest migration keeps current behavior through catalogs first, then progressively wires real blueprint-backed resolution.
planning_utils.py is still useful, but only for technical utilities, not fachliche template ownership.
routes/teams.py should stop being the source of large seed blueprint data and should delegate to a seed catalog service.
Blueprint metadata should travel into PlanNodes/Tasks so worker routing, RAG and verification can use role/template intent without client-side orchestration.
This track directly supports the desired role/template -> team -> blueprint mechanism becoming the single source of planning truth.

notes: Dependency validation result: all depends_on references resolve to in-track task IDs.
Milestone coverage validation result: all tasks are covered exactly once across milestone task_ids.
Updated stale summary timestamps and kept counters aligned with task status distribution.
Added explicit stage-level execution summary to support milestone-based progress tracking.
Added scope rules to guard architecture, governance and migration boundaries during implementation.

end_summaries.title: Analysis result
items: Task graph is structurally valid: no dangling dependencies and no uncovered milestone tasks.
Track already had clear purpose/success criteria, but lacked stage-level progress structure and final summaries.

end_summaries.title: Corrections
items: tasks_status_summary.updated_at was refreshed to the current analysis timestamp.
Consistency checkpoints were documented explicitly in analysis_checks.

end_summaries.title: Improvements and extensions
items: Added execution_stage_summary with per-milestone total/done/todo/in_progress/blocked counters.
Added scope_rules to make architecture and policy guardrails explicit for this cleanup track.
Added structured end_summaries for compact reporting at the end of the file.

end_summaries.title: End status
items: Current state: 17 tasks (17 todo, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked, 0 done).
All six milestones are prepared and currently in todo state; implementation can start with PBC-M1.
